rag_rules: AGREE annex blocks must be placed in valid AADL scopes: component type annexes for assumptions/guarantees, implementation annexes for bindings.
Assumptions and guarantees referencing component ports must be in component type scopes; implementation-level bindings go in implementation scopes.
generic_rules: Preserve AADL structural integrity when inserting AGREE annexes; ensure all referenced identifiers exist in the model.
Use component type scopes for contract-level declarations unless implementation-specific bindings are required.
Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type scopes or vice versa.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Component ports referenced in AGREE contracts must be valid in the scope where the annex is placed.
anti_patterns: Avoid placing implementation-level AGREE bindings in component type scopes.
Do not reference subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless properly represented through variables or outputs.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations as AGREE variables
Boolean data range comparisons with opaque types
Type mismatch diagnostics for assumed vs actual types
